assertion_results.text: Investigation identifies two independent root causes: (1) convention reference formatter using wrong case transform, (2) task creation using Feature issue type ID instead of Task (Steps 2-4)
assertion_results.evidence: investigation.md Step 3 Root Cause 1 (lines 90-95): 'Convention Reference Formatter (shared/convention-utils.md)' — 'The convention reference formatter applies a lowercase + kebab-case transform to CONVENTIONS.md section headings'. Root Cause 2 (lines 97-102): 'Task Creation Issue Type (plan-feature/SKILL.md Step 6a)' — 'reads the Feature issue type ID (10142) from Jira Configuration when creating task issues, instead of using a dedicated Task issue type ID value'. Step 4 (lines 118-122) summarizes both as independent defects. Steps 2, 3, and 4 all consistently identify both root causes.

assertion_results.text: Decomposition Guard is triggered because the two root causes are in different modules/code paths (Step 6)
assertion_results.evidence: investigation.md Independence Assessment (lines 105-112): 'These two root causes are independent: They reside in different modules: shared/convention-utils.md vs. plan-feature/SKILL.md. They affect different code paths: convention reference formatting vs. Jira issue creation.' Decomposition Trigger (lines 130-132): 'Because these are two independent root causes in different modules, the Decomposition Guard (Step 6) is triggered.' The decomposition-guard.md file exists as the Step 6 output confirming the guard was triggered.

assertion_results.text: Decomposition Guard presents both issues with their affected files/modules (Step 6)
assertion_results.evidence: decomposition-guard.md line 5: '1. Convention reference formatter uses wrong case transform (in shared/convention-utils.md)' and line 7: '2. Task creation uses wrong issue type ID (in plan-feature/SKILL.md Step 6a)'. Both issues are presented with their specific affected file paths.

assertion_results.text: Decomposition Guard offers the user two options: Proceed with single Task or Split into separate Bugs (Step 6)
assertion_results.evidence: decomposition-guard.md lines 11-14: 'Options: 1. Proceed -- create a single Task covering all fixes 2. Split -- I recommend creating separate Bugs for each independent issue, then triaging each one individually with /triage-bug'. Both options explicitly offered.

assertion_results.text: The skill does NOT silently create a single Task bundling both fixes — it waits for user input (Step 6 stop rule)
assertion_results.evidence: decomposition-guard.md ends with 'Choose (1/2):' on line 15, explicitly waiting for user input. No task creation output files exist in the outputs directory (only investigation.md and decomposition-guard.md). investigation.md line 132 also states: 'the user must decide whether to proceed with one combined Task or split into separate Bugs.'
